updates.summary: Community members held a massive public meeting regarding the CoreSite DE3 data center being built in the Globeville/Elyria-Swansea neighborhood. CoreSite pulled out of the meeting at the last minute citing safety concerns, enraging hundreds of neighbors who showed up anyway. The overflow rooms were completely full with the outside crowd pounding on windows. The GES Coalition demands transparency around power/water use, environmental impact study, disclosure around diesel backup generator use, and high quality generators to limit emissions.

Linn County passed a new ordinance requiring data center builders to conduct environmental reviews, limit light pollution and noise, and compensate the county for infrastructure costs. This was triggered by Google informing the county in October 2025 of plans for a six-building campus in Palo alongside the Duane Arnold Energy Center (Iowa's sole nuclear plant). Residents at public hearings raised concerns about water use, electricity rates, light pollution, and low-frequency noise impacts on livestock. Some called for a complete moratorium.

Chatham County approved a 12-month moratorium on the development of new data centers and cryptocurrency mining facilities on February 13, 2026. The ban applies to all development approvals for data centers, data processing facilities, cryptocurrency mining operations and 'any uses associated with data processing facilities,' including web services, hosting, and genome sequencing. The moratorium applies to unincorporated areas and lasts through February 2027.

Gates County's Board of Commissioners unanimously approved a one-year moratorium on data centers in December 2025, the first NC county to do so. The ordinance prohibits accepting, processing, or approving applications for data centers, cryptocurrency mining, or other high-impact data processing facilities until December 17, 2026. Triggered by two inquiries from PACE, a firm that promotes data center development.

TigerDC formally withdrew its $3 billion Project Spero data center proposal from Spartanburg County after the county council voted not to offer a tax incentive following weeks of strong public opposition. Hundreds of residents showed up at a public hearing against the project, and thousands signed a petition. The 'No Data Centers in Spartanburg' Facebook page organized community opposition. County council stated it would not vote on any new data center projects until the state legislature establishes statewide rules.

QTS is building a nine-building $1 billion campus near Lake Wylie, threatening the 95-acre Penland Christmas Tree Farm next door. Residents are concerned about noise, blinding security lights, and the sale of 400+ acres under the secretive code name 'Project COBRA' without community knowledge. Nearly 800 people signed a petition to stop the project. The tree farm owner worries increased heat could dry out his trees and ruin a nearly 60-year-old family business.

Montour County commissioners unanimously denied Talen Energy's request to rezone 800+ acres from agricultural to industrial for a data center and power plant expansion near Montour Preserve. Commission chair stated 'No evidence was provided demonstrating that the benefits outweigh documented and foreseeable public harms.' The Concerned Citizens of Montour County grew from 4 members in August 2025 to hundreds, with ~3,000 petition signatures. Talen says it still plans to develop in the county.

Lackawanna County Commissioner Bill Gaughan asked Governor Shapiro and the PA General Assembly for a three-year statewide moratorium on new large-scale data center approvals. The county alone has at least 11 proposed data center campuses, six in Archbald. State Senator Rosemary Brown announced support for a moratorium and unveiled a legislative package to restrict data centers to industrial zones. State Representatives Kyle Mullins and Kyle Donahue also back the legislation.

Residents continue to fight the $7 billion Stargate 1.4 GW data center despite MPSC approval of DTE's 19-year power supply agreement in December 2025. In February 2026, a judge denied resident Kathryn Haushalter's motion to intervene in the settlement between the developer and township. However, residents filed a separate mandamus complaint against Saline Township on January 28. Residents have called the project 'uniquely evil' citing environmental degradation, air and water pollution, and distrust of tech companies.

Maryland lawmakers pushed forward a data center impact report, overriding Governor Moore's veto. The state is conducting a comprehensive study on data center impacts expected to conclude in September 2026. Multiple regulation bills are being considered in the 2026 session, including requirements for environmental review and ratepayer protections.

Oklahoma SB 1488 proposes a moratorium on building new data centers until November 1, 2029, while directing the Oklahoma Corporation Commission to study impacts on water supply, utility rates, property values, and siting. Sponsored by Republican state Sen. Kendal Sacchieri. A separate bill, HB 2992 (Data Center Consumer Ratepayer Protection Act), requires data centers to pay infrastructure costs and passed the House Utilities Policy Committee 7-0.

Virginia is considering 60+ data center bills in the 2026 session. Key proposals: HB1515 would impose a temporary moratorium until July 2028 or until all existing interconnection requests are fulfilled. The Senate budget proposes phasing out the sales tax exemption (worth $1.6B/year) on Jan 1, 2027 - expedited from the original 2035 end date. A CCAN poll found 75% of voters blame data centers for rising utility costs and 9 in 10 believe lawmakers need to take action.
